summary.intent: Update the web UI to display compaction metadata (mode, token usage, settings hint) that was already being broadcast on the backend in PR #653.
summary.outcome: Both JS frontend files and the Rust chat service were updated: renderCompactCard now shows mode/tokens/hint, the websocket router handles both 'compact' and 'auto_compact' done events with a shared debounce, a notify_channels_of_compaction helper was added to chat/src/lib.rs and wired into both compact call sites, and 3 new unit tests were added. All checks passed (fmt, clippy, 221 chat tests).

repo: Compaction events are broadcast on the 'chat' channel with state='compact' (manual RPC) or state='auto_compact' (context window exceeded); both use phase='done' to signal completion.
The channel notification pattern follows send_retry_status_to_channels as a model — a dedicated format_channel_compaction_notice helper was the idiomatic approach.
RPC response shape for chat.compact intentionally returns only the history array (serde_json::json!(compacted)); all metadata flows via broadcast events, so no RPC contract change was needed.
The send() pre-emptive compact path goes through ChatService::compact, so wiring notify_channels_of_compaction there covers both the RPC and pre-emptive cases.
Structured compaction can fall back to RecencyPreserving on LLM failure; the broadcast reports the effective mode, not the requested mode.
Biome is used for JS linting/formatting in this repo.
Compaction mode keys in broadcast events use snake_case matching moltis.toml serialization (e.g., 'recency_preserving', 'structured').

code.finding: renderCompactCard was updated to display mode, token counts (input + output + total), and a settings hint. Inline styling was used instead of new CSS classes to avoid adding ctx-row-full or similar classes that didn't exist.

Both 'auto_compact' done and 'compact' done events route to renderCompactCard. A shared debounce was added to prevent duplicate cards when send() triggers a pre-emptive compact (which also fires an auto_compact event).

Added format_channel_compaction_notice helper and notify_channels_of_compaction, wired into ChatService::compact and the run_with_tools auto-compact path. Three unit tests added: format_channel_compaction_notice_structured, format_channel_compaction_notice_recency, and compaction_mode_key_matches_toml_serialization.

workflow: When adding new CSS classes, verify they exist in the stylesheet first (checked ctx-row-full and ctx classes) — if absent, use inline styles instead.
Run Biome before committing JS changes; auto-fixes may be needed.
Clippy should be run in both default features and --no-default-features configurations before pushing.

friction: Initial CSS class (ctx-row-full) used in renderCompactCard didn't exist, requiring a fallback to inline styling.
The debounce for auto_compact and compact events needed to be unified to prevent duplicate card renders on the send() pre-emptive path — not immediately obvious from the existing code structure.
open_items: Frontend could detect mode mismatch (requested_mode !== effective_mode) when Structured falls back to RecencyPreserving and show a more specific message like 'Structured fell back to RecencyPreserving'.
UI settings panel integration for changing chat.compaction.mode is referenced in the settingsHint but not yet built.
Greptile re-index was pending at time of push for PR #653.
